mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales indicates rising household demand and stronger near-term revenue expectations for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is sustained.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value 344380.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if rolling 3-month RSAFS growth has nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year walk-forward window or if positive-trend months do not outperform negative-trend months.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals weakening labor income and recession risk, causing investors to mark down expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value 8.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.5% and CPI accelerates, rising inflation lowers real household purchasing power without a conventional rate-cut offset, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value 0.18 as of t-1mo
fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value 212.714 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the FEDFUNDS-below-0.5 and CPI-acceleration interaction has a nonnegative coefficient in walk-forward return regressions or if triggered 60-day returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in industrial production signals improving real activity and operating leverage for cyclical firms, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO snapshot value 97.3755 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 returns across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels signals improving credit availability and durable-goods demand, lifting construction-linked earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 700 and HOUST rises > 5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST snapshot value 583.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing rebounds are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations or if average triggered returns are not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment remains above 7% but payrolls rise over 3 months, the economy is in early labor recovery with ample slack, supporting earnings growth without immediate wage inflation and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value 8.5 as of t-1mo
fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot value 133019.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the high-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS interaction fails to produce higher 20-day returns than either condition alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase greater than 25 basis points over 3 months signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value 0.18 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day S&P 500 returns after triggered FEDFUNDS increases are positive in more than 60% of cases or if drawdowns are not larger than unconditional 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real GDP and retail sales identifies broad demand contraction, causing earnings revisions and risk appetite to weaken, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0 and RSAFS 3-month change < 0
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:RSAFS
fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot value 11522.1 as of t-6mo
fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value 344380.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-and-RSAFS contraction signal does not have a negative 60-day return coefficient or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns in walk-forward testing.
